query: What dental services are not covered?
answer:
The plan does not cover dental services related to motor vehicle accidents, personal injuries, commercial liabilities, or homeowner policies.

| Topic | Coverage Information |
| :--- | :--- |
| Exclusions And Limitations | The plan does not provide benefits for any of the following conditions, treatments, services, or for any direct complications or consequences thereof. The plan does not provide benefits for excluded services even if approved, prescribed, or recommended by a Participating Provider. • Benefits aren’t available under this plan when coverage is available through: • Motor vehicle medical/dental or motor vehicle no-fault coverage • Personal injury protection (PIP) coverage • Commercial liability coverage • Homeowner policy • Other types of liability insurance • Worker's Compensation or similar coverage • Bridges, crowns, dentures, or prosthetic devices requiring multiple treatment dates or fittings, if the prosthetic item is installed or delivered more than 60 days after termination of coverage. • The completion or delivery of treatments or services performed or initiated prior to the member’s effective date, including the following: • Endodontic services and prosthodontic services; • An appliance or modification of one, if an impression for it was made prior to the member’s effective date; or • A crown, bridge, or cast or processed restoration, if the tooth was prepared prior to the member’s effective date. • Such services are the liability of the member, prior dental plan, and provider. • Endodontic therapy completed more than 60 days after termination of coverage. • Exams or consultations needed solely in connection with a service that is not covered. • Experimental or Investigational services and related exams or consultations. 14 Willamette Dental Plan January 1, 2024 1000016 • Full mouth reconstruction, including the extensive restoration of the mouth with crowns, or bridges. • Hospitalization care outside of a dental office for dental procedures, physician services, or facility fees. See Hospital Setting for a description of when the services of a dentist or other provider are covered when you are hospitalized. • Maxillofacial prosthetic services. • Nightguards. • Orthognathic surgery procedures to lengthen or shorten the jaw not required due to temporomandibular joint disorder, injury, sleep apnea or congenital anomaly. • Personalized restorations. • Plastic, reconstructive, or cosmetic surgery and other services, which are primarily intended to improve, alter, or enhance appearance. • Prescription and over-the-counter drugs and pre-medications. • Provider charges for a missed appointment or cancelled appointment without 24 hours prior notice. •... |
